description: Build a Bun + Fastify WebSocket gateway for AdaptiveAgent with deterministic routing, gateway-owned sessions, run orchestration, channel fanout, hooks, and scheduled ingress.

userStories.title: Scaffold gateway-fastify workspace
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated packages/gateway-fastify workspace so the WebSocket gateway can be implemented independently from the core runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: Create packages/gateway-fastify with package name @adaptive-agent/gateway-fastify
Add Bun scripts for build, test, and typecheck
Add a TypeScript package entrypoint and minimal server bootstrap stub
Running bun run --cwd packages/gateway-fastify build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define gateway and agent config schemas
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want explicit config schemas for gateway and agent JSON files so startup validation is deterministic and actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TypeScript types and validators for gateway config and agent config files
Agent config requires stable id, name, invocationModes, defaultInvocationMode, and model configuration
Invalid config reports actionable startup errors
Add tests for valid and invalid config loading
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add module registries for tools delegates hooks and auth
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want explicit registries for executable behavior so JSON config stays declarative and safe.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement registries that resolve configured tool, delegate, hook, and auth provider references
Unknown registry references fail fast with actionable errors
Hook and auth loading do not evaluate arbitrary inline code from JSON
Add tests for successful and failed registry resolution
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build lazy agent registry
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want agent configs materialized lazily into AdaptiveAgent instances so startup remains predictable and agent identity is cached by id.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent registry loads validated agent configs and exposes them by agentId
AdaptiveAgent instances are created lazily on first use and cached
Registry exposes invocation capability metadata needed by the gateway
Add tests for lazy instantiation and cache reuse
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define WebSocket protocol types and validation
userStories.description: As a client integrator, I want stable frame contracts so the gateway protocol is explicit and rejects malformed traffic consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Define inbound frame types for session.open, message.send, run.start, approval.resolve, channel.subscribe, session.close, and ping
Define outbound frame types for session.opened, session.updated, agent.event, message.output, run.output, approval.requested, error, and pong
Malformed or unknown frames are rejected with stable protocol error codes
Add tests for protocol validation and error mapping
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bootstrap Fastify WebSocket server
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the gateway server to start on Bun with a configured WebSocket endpoint so clients can connect to a real transport.
acceptanceCriteria: Start Fastify on the configured host, port, and websocketPath
WebSocket connections are wired to the validated frame protocol
Optional health endpoint returns service status when configured
Add a server bootstrap smoke test or integration test
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Authenticate upgrades with JWT
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want JWT authentication on WebSocket upgrade so every session runs under an explicit trusted principal.
acceptanceCriteria: Validate bearer JWTs before session creation unless a channel is configured as public
Normalize auth context with subject, optional tenantId, roles, and claims
Expired or invalid tokens return stable auth errors
Reconnect requires a fresh valid JWT rather than reusing expired authorization
Add tests for valid, invalid, and expired token flows
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add gateway store interfaces and in-memory stores
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want gateway-owned store interfaces with in-memory implementations so session and cron state can be built independently of the runtime stores.
acceptanceCriteria: Define store interfaces for sessions, transcript messages, session-run links, cron jobs, and cron runs
Provide in-memory implementations for tests and local development
Gateway stores remain authoritative for client and session facts instead of duplicating runtime execution facts
Add tests for the in-memory store behaviors
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement session open and reattach lifecycle
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want to create or reopen a session across reconnects so my gateway conversation state persists for the same principal.
acceptanceCriteria: session.open creates a new session or reattaches to an existing session for the same principal
Session records include channelId, principal ownership, status, transcript version, timestamps, and active run pointers
Different principals cannot attach to an existing session
Multiple same-principal connections can attach to observe the same session
Add tests for create, reattach, and ownership rejection flows
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement deterministic bindings router
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want routing decided by bindings and defaults so the gateway chooses the correct agent without model involvement.
acceptanceCriteria: Routing evaluates session pinning before channel, tenant, role, and default bindings
Normal chat traffic cannot override routing by supplying agentId directly
Session records retain their resolved agentId unless an explicit host rebind occurs
Add tests for binding priority and fallback behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist transcript history and replay envelope
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the gateway to own transcript replay so multi-turn chat can work with the current agent.chat contract without unbounded history growth.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist session transcript messages in gateway storage
Build chat replay envelopes from recent raw messages plus a rolling summary field
Summary refresh triggers are configurable and run after successful turns or when the recent window exceeds a threshold
Add tests for transcript persistence and replay envelope composition
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute chat turns through agent.chat
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want message.send to run a conversational turn against the routed agent so I can have a persistent multi-turn chat session.
acceptanceCriteria: message.send loads the session replay envelope, appends the inbound user message, and invokes agent.chat
Persist run linkage for the resulting root run on the session
Successful assistant output is appended to the transcript and delivered via message.output or session frames
Failed turns preserve prior transcript history and update session status consistently
Add tests for successful and failed chat turns
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Execute structured runs through agent.run
userStories.description: As an integrator, I want run.start requests to invoke agent.run so structured commands and automation can execute without chat transcript semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: run.start validates that the target agent and endpoint allow run mode
Build a RunRequest from goal, optional input, optional context, and metadata
Persist runId, rootRunId, and session linkage for session-bound structured runs
Return terminal run output through the gateway response path
Add tests for session-bound and isolated structured runs
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resume paused runs from approval frames
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want approval.resolve to continue a paused run so approval-required work can resume without starting over.
acceptanceCriteria: approval.resolve is accepted only when the session has an authorized pending runId
Approval resolution calls resolveApproval and resume on the same run rather than creating a new root run
Session status moves back to running, idle, or failed based on the resumed result
Add tests for approve, reject, and unauthorized approval flows
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce single-writer session concurrency
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want one active root run and one write lane per session so multiple tabs or devices cannot race gateway state.
acceptanceCriteria: Only one active root run is allowed per session
Additional message.send and run.start frames are rejected while a session is running
Only approval-related writes are accepted while a session is awaiting_approval
Same-principal observers can remain attached without gaining a second write lane
Add tests for running and awaiting_approval concurrency rules
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bridge runtime events onto gateway channels
userStories.description: As a client developer, I want channel subscriptions and correlated runtime events so I can render progress for sessions and runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement channel subscriptions for at least session, run, root-run, and agent scopes
Bridge runtime events such as run.created, run.status_changed, tool.started, tool.completed, delegate.spawned, approval.requested, approval.resolved, run.completed, run.failed, and snapshot.created
Outbound events include correlation metadata such as sessionId, agentId, runId, rootRunId, and parentRunId when available
Add tests for subscription fanout and event correlation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Authorize outbound delivery and redact sensitive metadata
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want outbound events filtered and redacted so clients only receive authorized data.
acceptanceCriteria: Channel subscriptions are authorized against the caller auth context
Sensitive auth claims are redacted before outbound delivery
Unauthorized channel deliveries are dropped or rejected with stable errors
Add tests for outbound authorization and redaction behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add gateway hook lifecycle
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want lifecycle hooks around gateway actions so auth policy, rate limits, auditing, and outbound filtering can be extended safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement configured hook slots for onAuthenticate, onSessionResolve, beforeRoute, beforeInboundMessage, beforeRunStart, afterRunResult, onAgentEvent, beforeOutboundFrame, onDisconnect, and onError
before hooks can reject or enrich request metadata
Hook failure policy supports fail, warn, and ignore
Add tests for hook ordering and failure policy behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement file-backed gateway persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a durable single-node storage backend so sessions and scheduled work survive restarts during local development.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a file-backed store for sessions, transcript messages, session-run links, cron jobs, and cron runs
Use a documented on-disk layout that matches the logical gateway artifacts
Session and cron state can be reloaded after process restart in local development
Add tests for file-backed persistence and reload behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Postgres session transcript and linkage stores
userStories.description: As an operator, I want PostgreSQL-backed gateway state for sessions and transcripts so production deployments have durable multi-process storage.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Postgres-backed persistence for gateway sessions, transcript messages, and session-run links
Session and transcript queries preserve principal ownership, status, active run pointers, and turn ordering
Runtime execution facts remain authoritative in runtime stores while gateway facts stay in gateway tables
Add tests for the Postgres store contract or SQL mapping layer
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Postgres cron store and lease claiming
userStories.description: As an operator, I want durable cron jobs with lease-based claiming so scheduled work executes once per fire time across multiple workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Postgres-backed persistence for cron jobs and cron runs
Implement lease-based claiming so only one worker can execute a due fire time
Claimed jobs record run history and terminal status
Add tests for lease claim and release behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add scheduler loop and cron target dispatch
userStories.description: As an operator, I want due cron jobs to flow through the same orchestrator as live traffic so scheduled work behaves like normal gateway work.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a scheduler loop that claims due jobs and dispatches them through the shared orchestrator
Support cron target kinds session_event, isolated_run, and isolated_chat
session_event uses the session flow while isolated targets avoid inheriting arbitrary user transcript state
Add tests for scheduler dispatch and target-kind mapping
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add cron delivery modes and approval policy
userStories.description: As an operator, I want scheduled results delivered predictably so cron executions can write back to sessions, announce summaries, or notify external systems.
acceptanceCriteria: Support cron delivery modes session, announce, webhook, and none
Webhook delivery posts a completion payload after the run result is finalized
Cron executions that reach approval state are marked failed or needs-review instead of blocking indefinitely
Add tests for delivery-mode routing and approval policy behavior
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restore active sessions on reconnect
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I want reconnecting sockets to recover active session state so pending approvals and current status remain visible after transport interruptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Reattached connections receive the current session status and active run linkage
Pending approval sessions surface their outstanding run state after reconnect
Observers rejoin the correct authorized channels after reconnect
Add tests for reconnect recovery with idle, running, and awaiting_approval sessions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add gateway health checks metrics and structured logs
userStories.description: As an operator, I want operational signals for the gateway so startup failures, degraded backends, and runtime activity are observable.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose health or readiness surfaces for normal, startup-failed, and degraded states
Emit structured logs for auth failures, routing decisions, session lifecycle, run dispatch, hook failures, and cron activity
Track basic counters for sessions, active runs, auth failures, routing misses, and cron claims
Add tests for health-state reporting and logging integration points where practical
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document gateway config protocol and local operations
userStories.description: As a developer or operator, I want documentation for the gateway so I can run it locally, configure routing, and integrate against the WebSocket protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: Document gateway and agent config structure with example JSON files
Document the supported inbound and outbound WebSocket frames
Document local development flow for in-memory, file-backed, and Postgres-backed modes
Document scheduler behavior, delivery modes, and reconnect expectations
Typecheck passes
